title: Gonostemon gariepensis

This classification is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics, which align with those of other members of the Asparagaceae family. More specifically, Gonostemon gariepensis is part of the subfamily Scilloideae, which includes plants that are often bulbous and produce showy flowers. Within the subfamily Scilloideae, Gonostemon gariepensis is likely part of the tribe Scilleae, although further research may be needed to confirm this classification. intro: Gonostemon gariepensis, also known as Hoodia gordonii's close relative, is a succulent plant native to southern Africa.
